name: Ascend Scheduler Interface
description: Cron parsing, next-run computation, and launchd plist generation for scheduled task management. Converts human-friendly schedule specifications to cron expressions, computes next run times, and generates/manages macOS launchd plist files for scheduling.

types.name: CronFields

fields.name: dom
fields.description: Day of month field (1-31, *, or range)

fields.name: dow
fields.description: Day of week field (0-6, *, or range, 0=Sunday in cron)

types.description: Dictionary containing parsed cron expression fields

functions.name: schedule_to_cron
functions.description: Convert human-friendly schedule spec to a 5-field cron expression (minute hour day-of-month month day-of-week)

inputs.name: weekly
inputs.description: Day name for weekly schedule (sun/mon/tue/wed/thu/fri/sat or full name)

error_cases.name: unknown_day
error_cases.condition: weekly or biweekly day name not in _DAY_MAP

error_cases.name: no_frequency
error_cases.condition: No schedule frequency parameter is truthy

preconditions: At least one schedule frequency flag must be True or set
If weekly or biweekly is set, day name must be valid (sun/mon/tue/wed/thu/fri/sat or full name)
Only one schedule frequency should be specified
postconditions: Returns valid 5-field cron expression string

functions.name: parse_cron
functions.description: Parse a 5-field cron expression into components dictionary with keys: minute, hour, dom, month, dow

error_cases.name: invalid_field_count
error_cases.condition: expr does not contain exactly 5 fields

preconditions: expr must contain exactly 5 whitespace-separated fields
postconditions: Returns dict with keys: minute, hour, dom, month, dow
Each value is a string (may contain *, ranges, lists)

functions.name: _expand_field
functions.description: Expand a cron field into a set of integer values. Handles wildcards (*), ranges (1-5), and comma-separated lists.

inputs.name: field
inputs.description: Cron field to expand (*, ranges, or integers)

error_cases.name: invalid_int
error_cases.condition: field contains non-integer values when not * or range

preconditions: field contains valid cron syntax (*, ranges, or integers)
postconditions: Returns set of integers within [min_val, max_val]
* expands to full range
Ranges like '1-5' expand to {1,2,3,4,5}
Comma-separated values are combined

functions.name: compute_next_run
functions.description: Compute next run time from a cron expression using proper cron semantics (OR logic for dom/dow when both specified). Returns ISO format datetime string or empty string if no match found within 366 days.

inputs.name: after
inputs.description: Starting datetime to search from (defaults to now)

preconditions: cron_expr is a valid 5-field cron expression
postconditions: Returns datetime string in format '%Y-%m-%d %H:%M' if match found within 366 days
Returns empty string if no match found within 366 days
Returned time is strictly after the 'after' parameter
side_effects: Calls datetime.now() if after is None

structured_side_effects.description: Reads current system time if after parameter is None

functions.name: describe_cron
functions.description: Return a human-readable description of a cron expression. Falls back to returning the raw expression for complex cases.

error_cases.name: invalid_int_conversion
error_cases.condition: hour or minute field cannot be converted to int for simple cases

preconditions: cron_expr is a valid 5-field cron expression
postconditions: Returns human-readable description or raw cron_expr for complex cases

functions.name: _find_ascend_path
functions.description: Find the ascend executable path by checking PATH, then common install locations. Returns 'ascend' as fallback.
postconditions: Returns path to ascend executable if found
Returns 'ascend' as fallback if not found

structured_side_effects.target: System PATH and common installation directories
structured_side_effects.description: Searches for ascend executable in PATH and filesystem

functions.name: _cron_to_calendar_intervals
functions.description: Convert cron expression to launchd CalendarInterval XML format. Generates simplified intervals for common patterns.
preconditions: cron_expr is a valid 5-field cron expression
postconditions: Returns XML string representing CalendarInterval dict or array
Single interval returns <dict>, multiple intervals return <array>

functions.name: generate_plist
functions.description: Generate launchd plist metadata (label, ascend_path, log_path) for a schedule. Note: actual implementation returns tuple, not plist content string.
postconditions: Returns tuple of (label, ascend_path, log_path)

functions.name: write_plist
functions.description: Write a launchd plist file to ~/Library/LaunchAgents/ and return its path. Creates directory if needed.
preconditions: cron_expr is a valid 5-field cron expression
postconditions: Plist file written to ~/Library/LaunchAgents/com.ascend.schedule.{schedule_name}.plist
Directory ~/Library/LaunchAgents created if not exists
Returns Path to written plist file

functions.name: load_plist
functions.description: Load a launchd plist using launchctl. Returns True on success, False otherwise.
postconditions: Returns True if plist exists and launchctl load succeeds
Returns False if plist doesn't exist or load fails

structured_side_effects.description: Executes system command to load launchd plist

functions.name: unload_plist
functions.description: Unload a launchd plist using launchctl. Returns True on success, False otherwise.
postconditions: Returns True if plist exists and launchctl unload succeeds
Returns False if plist doesn't exist or unload fails

structured_side_effects.description: Executes system command to unload launchd plist

functions.name: remove_plist
functions.description: Unload and remove a launchd plist file. Returns True if file was removed, False otherwise.
postconditions: Plist file is unloaded (if loaded)
Plist file is deleted from filesystem (if exists)
Returns True if file existed and was removed
Returns False if file didn't exist

structured_side_effects.description: Unloads the plist via launchctl command

invariants: _DAY_MAP maps day names (full and abbreviated) to cron weekday values (0=Sunday)
_CRON_WEEKDAY_TO_PYTHON maps cron weekday (0=Sunday) to Python weekday (0=Monday)
Cron expressions are always 5 fields: minute hour dom month dow
Biweekly schedules target days 8-14 and 22-28 to approximate 2nd and 4th occurrences
Quarterly schedules run on first day of months 1,4,7,10
When both dom and dow are restricted (not *), cron uses OR semantics (match either)
compute_next_run searches up to 366 days ahead for next match
